role_preferences.recent_work_context: Training machine learning models based on onboarding graph linkage vector features.
role_preferences.anticipated_start_date_preference: One month out from the current date
role_preferences.location_and_relocation_rule: The candidate is open to relocating anywhere in the United States or Canada. For any yes/no 'willing to relocate' question, answer Yes. For location-selection widgets, defer to routing_priorities.location_selection_execution_rule (multi-select location lists: select ALL options).
role_preferences.work_arrangement_preference: Open to onsite, hybrid, or remote — will accept whatever the role requires.
role_preferences.work_arrangement_rule: Never disqualify or answer negatively on a work-arrangement question. The candidate accepts onsite, hybrid, or remote as required by the role; a remote/hybrid preference must not translate into declining an onsite role.

work_experience.role: Associate Fraud Risk Strategy Data Scientist
work_experience.location: 6220 America Center Dr Ste 100, San Jose, CA 95002
work_experience.description: Engineered a multi-objective NSGA-II genetic algorithm via Optuna to jointly optimize 15 production rules, processing 15,000 trials in 2 minutes and reducing manual referrals by 4.4% per quarter. Prototyped a KYC fraud detection system combining a Random Forest model (0.79 AUC), a NetworkX identity graph utilizing vectorized identity signals, and an agentic AI LiteLLM tool-calling loop to synthesize investigation tickets. Deployed an end-to-end AWS SageMaker batch ML pipeline utilizing graph-based fanout, achieving 99.5% recall and 94.5% precision on reachable fraud. Identified and resolved a portfolio-wide pipeline bug affecting 411,000 payment events across a 6 month window, enabling clean rule re-tuning. Developed automated KPI dashboards with Tableau, providing stakeholders with real-time visibility into fraud prevention trends, rule performance week-over-week, and loss prevention metrics.

Fine-tuned ChatGPT models for cryo-electron microscopy (cryoEM) workstations to improve AI-assisted scientific imaging workflows. Led the integration of AI capabilities into the company website, enabling direct user interaction with fine-tuned models for cryo-EM data analysis tasks.

Engineered and rigorously tested data science workflows tailored to various industry sectors, enhancing team insights into workstation performance. Conducted extensive benchmarking of workflows by simulating real-world industry use cases using Python and Scikit-Learn, improving efficiency by 20%.

routing_priorities.contextual_citizenship_and_export_rules: The candidate is a dual US and Canadian citizen and a US Person for ITAR/EAR purposes, fully authorized in both countries. Derive the job's country from job_listing_locations: if any location is in Canada AND none is in the US, treat as Canada-based; otherwise US-based (default US when empty/ambiguous). For US roles pick the option representing the United States (match any of 'United States', 'United States of America', 'USA', 'US') for citizenship/nationality/export-control fields; for Canada roles pick the Canada option. Always select the actual option text present — do not emit a fixed string. Do NOT invent Canadian education or work history — the profile is accurate for both countries.
onsite_us_location_priority_ladder: Bay Area (San Francisco, San Jose, Milpitas, Oakland, Palo Alto, Sunnyvale, Mountain View, Redwood City, Menlo Park, Santa Clara)
New York, NY / New York City
Washington DC / Arlington, VA / Northern Virginia
Anywhere else in the United States
remote_us_location_priority_ladder: Bay Area (San Francisco, San Jose, Milpitas, Oakland, Palo Alto, Sunnyvale, Mountain View, Redwood City, Menlo Park, Santa Clara)
New York, NY / New York City
Washington DC / Arlington, VA / Northern Virginia
Anywhere else in the United States
routing_priorities.location_selection_execution_rule: Single-select location dropdowns — follow this logic in order: (0) REMOTE CHECK: if any dropdown option matches Remote/Virtual/Work-from-Home/Remote-US/Remote-Canada, ALWAYS pick it first regardless of anything else. (1) LADDER SELECTION: determine which ladder to use based on the JOB LISTING ITSELF — if job_listing_locations contains any remote/virtual indicator OR the listing title/type is flagged remote-eligible, use the remote_* ladder; if the listing is strictly onsite, use the onsite_* ladder. Combine this with job country (derived per contextual_citizenship_and_export_rules; default US). This gives four possible ladders: remote_us, onsite_us, remote_canada, onsite_canada. (2) LISTING MATCH: within the chosen ladder, collect dropdown options that fuzzy-match any location in job_listing_locations and pick the highest-ranked matched option. (3) LADDER FALLBACK: if no listing location matched any dropdown option, walk the chosen ladder top-to-bottom and pick the highest-ranked entry whose keywords appear in a dropdown option. Multi-select/checkbox location lists: select ALL available options.

compensation_rules.range_midpoint_rule: For desired-salary fields: if job_listing_salary is provided (injected at runtime), use it as the source of truth — dropdown of ranges: pick the option containing or closest to that value; free number field: use its midpoint as a rounded integer. If job_listing_salary is null but the description contains an explicit range, use the range tied to the highest-cost listed location (prefer San Francisco / California tier when multiple shown) and compute the exact min-max midpoint as a rounded integer. If no range is available, use compensation_rules.baseline_target_pay.

regulatory_self_identification.veteran_status_selection: I am not a protected veteran
regulatory_self_identification.disability_status_rule: Answer affirmative on disability self-identification: choose whatever option indicates the candidate has (or previously had) a disability — i.e. the 'Yes' choice on Form CC-305, whatever its exact wording (any option beginning with 'Yes' referencing having/having had a disability). Never pick 'No' or 'I do not wish to answer'. Rationale: conditions such as depression and ADHD fall within CC-305 guidelines.

workday_qa_examples.question: Do you currently hold a security clearance? (e.g. SECRET, L, TS/SCI, Top Secret)
workday_qa_examples.notes: Candidate holds NO active clearance of any level. If options list specific clearance levels, pick the 'No' / 'I do not hold a clearance' option — NEVER any 'Yes, I hold...' option. Candidate IS a US citizen and eligible to obtain a clearance if sponsored by an employer, but does not currently hold one.

workday_qa_examples.question: Are you eligible to obtain a security clearance?
workday_qa_examples.notes: US citizen — eligible to obtain a clearance if sponsored. Distinct from currently holding one, which is No.

workday_qa_examples.answer: Currently employed — please do not contact my current employer.
workday_qa_examples.notes: Works at BILL (San Jose, CA). For 3-way option sets, pick the option meaning 'employed / do NOT contact current employer'. Prior employers may be contacted freely.

workday_qa_examples.question: Are you a current or former federal government employee?

workday_qa_examples.notes: For option sets offering 'A current federal employee' / 'A former federal employee' / 'Not a current or former federal employee', pick 'Not a current or former federal employee'.
